title: ROSÉ and Bruno Mars Make Grammy History with 'APT.' Performance
text: ROSÉ and Bruno Mars delivered a high-energy performance of their hit single "APT." at the 68th Grammy Awards, marking a significant milestone for the K-pop artist. The performance was groundbreaking as "ROSÉ made history at the Grammys" by becoming the first solo K-pop star to ever perform at the annual ceremony. Additionally, she achieved another first by becoming "the first K-Pop artist to receive a nomination as a main performer in one of the top four categories." The track served as the lead single from ROSÉ's debut solo album Rosie, which was released in 2024. The song achieved massive commercial success, reaching number one on charts across 50 different countries. "APT." earned three major nominations: Song of the Year, Record of the Year, and Best Pop Duo/Group Performance. However, ROSÉ did not win in any of these categories. Kendrick Lamar and SZA took home Record of the Year for "Luther," while Billie Eilish won Song of the Year, and Cynthia Erivo with Ariana Grande won Best Pop Duo/Group Performance. During their stage appearance, ROSÉ performed vocals while Bruno Mars handled guitar duties, creating an energetic rendition that showcased both artists' talents.
